description: v1.2 private-scope delegation handed to a verifier who has no recipient device key. The verifier MUST fail closed with E_SCOPES_UNREADABLE per PRIVATE-SCOPE.md §2 step P5 — the absence of a decryption capability is a structural inability to evaluate authority, not a permissive default. Without the plaintext, the verifier cannot reconstruct the canonical message and cannot verify the BIP-322 signature.

expected.rejection_reason: The verifier holds device_id 'v17-bystander' (with the listed unrelated keypair), but the OC Agent envelope's scopes_encrypted only names 'v17-agent' as a recipient. No held key matches; the verifier cannot decrypt and so cannot evaluate the delegation.

harness_assertion: (1) Build the envelope by sealing to the v17-agent recipient ONLY. (2) Verify with decryptScopesWith = the bystander's keypair (which is NOT a recipient). (3) Result MUST be ok: false with code === 'E_SCOPES_UNREADABLE'. Implementations MAY ALSO accept E_BAD_LOCK_ENVELOPE if the underlying lock-core surfaces a different error class, since both fall under the broader 'I can't read this' bucket; conformant verifiers SHOULD prefer E_SCOPES_UNREADABLE for the specifically-this-key-doesn't-match case.
